question.text: A Market Maker in commodity derivatives is obligated to:

answer.text: Provide two-way quotes at all times to maintain liquidity
Take physical delivery of all commodity contracts
Guarantee profits to other market participants
Set the daily settlement price for the exchange

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A Market Maker is a class of members obligated to provide liquidity by giving two-way quotes (both buy and sell) at all times as prescribed by the exchange. **The Trap:** Market makers don't need to take physical delivery, don't guarantee profits, and don't set settlement prices — the exchange determines those. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII also covers the role of market makers in equity derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Market makers earn the bid-ask spread — they are essential for less liquid commodity contracts where natural buyers and sellers may not always be present.
